This sacrilege provoked the wrath of Jove, who destroyed the ship with thunderbolts and left Ulysses as the sole survivor, drifting for nine days until he was cast upon the island of Calypso.
050b7acea1a8ee84d829ba323704cdaa4d78835f: After receiving a lavish escort and parting from King Alcinous, Ulysses is carried by the Phaeacian ship to the shores of Ithaca, where he falls into a deep slumber. Unbeknownst to the Phaeacians, Neptune, angered by their hospitality toward Ulysses, turns their ship to stone and sinks it, prompting the Phaeacians to offer sacrifices to appease the god. Upon waking on his native soil, a disoriented Ulysses is met by Minerva, who reveals his true location and assists him in concealing his treasures in a sacred cave. To prepare him for the task of reclaiming his home from the insolent suitors, Minerva disguises him as a withered beggar, after which they devise a plan for vengeance while she departs to fetch his son, Telemachus.
f9fd1faa55f476abfdc722a2d99ba0a76af01260: Ulysses, disguised as a beggar, arrives at the hut of Eumaeus, a faithful and thrifty swineherd who has maintained his master's estate despite the absence of its lord. After being met with suspicion by the swineherd's fierce hounds, Ulysses is welcomed with traditional hospitality, where Eumaeus shares his grief over the presumed death of Ulysses and laments the greed of the suitors consuming his master's wealth. As they share a meal of roasted pork, Ulysses recounts his arduous journey, including his time in Egypt and his escape from shipwrecked perils, while Eumaeus remains skeptical of the stranger's claim that the great Ulysses is destined to return. Despite this disbelief, the two men form a bond of mutual respect, and the passage concludes with the swineherd providing the traveler with a place to sleep as a storm rolls in.
e571d339d1623afa01a21af7ebf7c11218f631b0: Minerva descends to Lacedaemon to urge Telemachus to return to Ithaca, warning him of the suitors' murderous intent and the danger to his estate. After receiving a hospitable farewell and precious gifts from Menelaus and Helen, Telemachus departs, accompanied by Pisistratus, and eventually encounters a fugitive seer named Theoclymenus, whom he takes aboard his ship. Meanwhile, the disguised Ulysses tests the loyalty of the swineherd Eumaeus, who recounts his own tragic history of being kidnapped by Phoenician traders. As Telemachus nears Ithaca, he leaves his ship and Theoclymenus under the care of his crew and heads toward Eumaeus's hut to inspect his herds, guided by omens of his future sovereignty.
7dae2ad7ecb9fcd3136a016705160b6fbfd2223b: At daybreak, the swineherd Eumaeus is astonished when his long-lost master, Ulysses, appears at his hut disguised as a traveler, only for the truth to be revealed when Telemachus arrives and the two share a tearful, joyous reunion. After Minerva restores Ulysses to his true, youthful appearance, the father and son devise a plan to reclaim their estate: Ulysses will return to the city in disguise to test the loyalty of the servants and women, while Telemachus prepares to arm himself for the coming conflict. Meanwhile, news of Telemachus's safe return reaches the palace, inciting the suitors to debate whether to kill him or proceed with their courtship of Penelope, who rebukes them for their insolence and plots against her son. As the tension mounts, Ulysses and Telemachus prepare for the inevitable confrontation with the many suitors who have been wasting their estate.
babff5625453d8b7e37bfcc3ff6a561d0811c33b: As rosy-fingered Dawn appeared, Telemachus departed for the city, leaving an elderly stranger in the care of the swineherd Eumaeus, while returning home to reunite with a weeping Penelope and share news of his travels and the uncertain fate of his father, Ulysses. Meanwhile, Eumaeus escorted the ragged stranger toward the town, where they were insulted and physically struck by the insolent Melanthius and the suitor Antinous. After the old dog Argos recognized Ulysses and died, the disguised king entered the house to beg among the suitors, enduring further abuse and a blow from Antinous, which deeply angered Telemachus and Penelope. Seeking news of her husband, Penelope requested to speak with the stranger, but he asked to wait until sundown to avoid the suitors' malice, leaving the household to await the evening's revelations.
23b430323673c2aece15f7eeafb7b4a16dc05c62: After the gluttonous tramp Irus insults Ulysses and attempts to drive him from the house, a brawl ensues in which Ulysses, bolstered by Minerva's strength, decisively defeats the younger man. Amidst the suitors' laughter and mockery, Ulysses delivers a somber warning to the well-meaning Amphinomus regarding the vanity of man and the impending doom facing those who dishonor a household. Meanwhile, Minerva enhances Penelope's beauty and composure, prompting her to appear before the suitors, where she skillfully solicits gifts while lamenting her plight and the greed of the men. Despite the growing insolence and verbal abuse from the suitors and servants, the tension is momentarily held in check by Telemachus and the tempering influence of Amphinomus, allowing the assembly to conclude their evening with drink-offerings.
33f164a739b54d0e6ddb3a8d02799eac680409f5: Under the guidance of Minerva, Ulysses and Telemachus secretly remove the house's armour to prepare for the coming slaughter of the suitors. While Telemachus secures the weapons, Ulysses remains in the cloister and later meets with Penelope, disguised as a stranger. Penelope, burdened by years of grief and the relentless pressure of suitors, tests the stranger's identity by asking for details about her husband's manner and his former travels. Ulysses provides indisputable proofs, including the description of a specific mantle and sword, leading Penelope to weep with hope despite her caution. During the encounter, the nurse Euryclea recognizes Ulysses by a distinctive scar on his leg received during a youthful hunt, but he silences her to protect his secret identity. The meeting concludes with Penelope sharing a prophetic dream of an eagle slaying her geese, which Ulysses confirms as a vision of the suitors' impending doom, leaving her to reflect on her uncertain future as she retires for the night.
14ad52ff41a9142b5a2a33ca0c4b63340555a98a: Restless and brooding, Ulysses lies awake in the cloister, wrestling with the fury to slay the suitors who consume his estate, until Minerva descends to reassure him of her divine protection and grant him sleep. Meanwhile, a grief-stricken Penelope prays to Diana for death to escape her misery, unaware of her husband's presence. As dawn breaks, Ulysses receives divine omens of impending vengeance through thunder and a miller-woman's words, while Telemachus prepares himself and the household for the suitors' arrival. Despite the insolence of the suitors—including Ctesippus, who throws an ox's foot at Ulysses—and their attempt to undermine Telemachus, the feast proceeds with a heavy sense of foreboding. The prophet Theoclymenus foretells disaster and leaves the house, leaving the suitors to feast amidst a growing, inexplicable gloom, unaware that their doom is near.
9071ded5dfe8330ac1d9ee6f78f12963ddbe6a91: Prompted by Minerva, Penelope challenges the suitors to a contest: whoever can string Ulysses' great bow and shoot an arrow through twelve axes shall win her hand. After the suitors and even Telemachus fail to string the mighty weapon, a disguised Ulysses reveals his true identity to his loyal servants, Eumaeus and Philoetius, preparing for the reckoning to come. Despite the fierce opposition and mockery of the ringleaders Antinous and Eurymachus, the disguised Ulysses is eventually given the bow, strings it with effortless grace, and pierces the twelve axes with a single shot. With a thunderous sign from Jove, the stage is set for the final confrontation as Telemachus stands armed beside his father.
e9898b6c2366fe28fd2285f2621873e32c89a42b: Having revealed himself, Ulysses unleashed a deadly onslaught upon the suitors, beginning with the fatal wounding of Antinous and continuing with a relentless barrage of arrows. Despite the suitors' attempts to arm themselves and negotiate, Ulysses and Telemachus, aided by the swineherd and stockman, fought with divine favor from Minerva to strike down nearly every intruder. After the slaughter, the men purged the house of the disloyal Melanthius and executed the twelve unfaithful maidservants by hanging them. Once the blood was cleansed and the cloisters purified with fire and sulphur, the long-awaited vengeance was complete, and Ulysses prepared to reunite with Penelope.
4ea5cf86b6a24c5e8133101ae8b555e5f61cb4e8: After Euryclea reveals the news of Ulysses' return and the slaughter of the suitors, a skeptical Penelope is hesitant to believe the truth, even when her nurse offers the proof of his secret scar. Seeking certainty, Penelope tests him by requesting their immovable olive-tree bed, a detail known only to them, which finally breaks her hesitation and brings the couple to a tearful, joyous reunion. As they share stories of their long separations and the trials they endured, Ulysses recounts his perilous journeys—from the Cyclops to Calypso—before preparing to face the social repercussions of the suitors' deaths. Under the protection of Minerva, he and his allies depart into the night to settle remaining accounts and visit his father, leaving Penelope to guard the household.
ddf97b2a76e1f8e92e85909662edfc9330b4a0ae: Mercury of Cyllene leads the whining ghosts of the suitors into Hades, where they encounter the shade of Agamemnon, who laments his treacherous end and commends the virtue of Penelope. The ghost of Amphimedon explains how the suitors were deceived by Penelope’s weaving and ultimately slaughtered by Ulysses and Telemachus. Meanwhile, on earth, Ulysses reunites with his aged father Laertes, proving his identity through the memory of a scar and the details of their vineyard. As the people of Ithaca gather to seek vengeance for the fallen suitors, a conflict erupts that is ultimately quelled by the intervention of Minerva, who, with the blessing of Jove, facilitates a covenant of peace to restore order to the land.
